hero.title: 5 Days — Best of Phu Quoc.
hero.titleAccent: Grand World, Starfish Island, Cable Car & Island Hopping.
hero.intro: Five days to see everything that makes Phu Quoc worth the flight. Day one: Ho Quoc Pagoda cliff temple and the Night Market. Day two: Starfish Beach (visible October–March) and Grand World Phu Quoc — the Venice canal city with a gondola, Bamboo Legend entertainment, and the Clock Tower. Day three: the four-island snorkelling circuit, Aquatopia Water Park, and the world's longest sea cable car (7.9 km) home. Day four: free for VinWonders or Vinpearl Safari. Day five: last morning on the beach.
hero.heroImageAlt: Phu Quoc Grand World Venice canal gondola island Vietnam

overview.heading: 5 Days Best of Phu Quoc — Grand World, Starfish, Island Hopping & Cable Car
body: This five-day package is the most comprehensive Phu Quoc itinerary — adding Grand World Phu Quoc and Starfish Island to the standard island hopping and cable car circuit that shorter packages cover. It is designed for travellers who want to see everything that has made Phu Quoc one of Southeast Asia's fastest-growing beach destinations, without rushing any single experience.

Grand World Phu Quoc is a night entertainment complex opened in 2021 on the northern coast of the island. The centrepiece is a recreated Venice canal with gondola rides, neon-reflected in the canal water. The Bamboo Legend zone has Vietnamese cultural performances, lantern street decorations, and food streets. The Clock Tower at Grand World's centre is the island's most distinctive modern landmark. The complex is best visited in the evening — arrive as the lights come on and the canal reflections begin.

Starfish Beach (Bai Sao Bien) at the northern tip of Phu Quoc is the only accessible location in Vietnam where live starfish in large numbers are visible in the shallows. The starfish (typically orange, purple, or red-spotted species) gather in the warm sandflat shallows near the beach and can be gently held and observed close up. The starfish are seasonal: they are visible from October to March when water temperatures and current conditions draw them to the shallows. Outside these months, the beach remains beautiful but the starfish are absent.

highlights.heading: Why This 5-Day Best of Phu Quoc Package Stands Apart

items.title: Grand World Phu Quoc — Venice Canal & Bamboo Legend
items.body: Grand World Phu Quoc is a riverside entertainment complex modelled on a classic European market town — specifically Venice's canal network. The central canal is lined with Italian-style townhouses whose ground floors are restaurants and cafés; gondola rides (RM35 per boat, optional) pass under the lantern-lit bridges. Bamboo Legend is the Vietnamese cultural zone adjacent to the canal — traditional architecture, folk performance stages, and lantern alleys. The Clock Tower at the canal's centre is lit in shifting colours after dark. Grand World is busiest and most atmospheric after 19:00.
items.imageAlt: Grand World Phu Quoc Venice canal gondola night Vietnam

items.title: Starfish Beach — Visible October to March
items.body: Starfish Beach at the northern tip of Phu Quoc is one of the most unusual natural experiences on the island — a shallow sandflat where large numbers of brightly coloured starfish (typically 20–30 cm diameter) gather in the warm water. The beach access is from a small pier; the starfish are visible in the first 20–50 metres of water at ankle-to-knee depth. They can be gently lifted and observed before being carefully returned. The species present — Linckia laevigata (blue), Protoreaster nodosus (chocolate chip), and others — are vibrant and intact. Available October to March only.
items.imageAlt: Starfish Beach Phu Quoc northern tip Vietnam October to March

items.title: World's Longest Sea Cable Car — 7.9 km
items.body: The Hon Thom Cable Car holds the Guinness World Record as the longest non-stop sea cable car: 7.9 km from the An Thoi terminal on Phu Quoc's mainland to Hon Thom (Pineapple Island). The gondola reaches 175 metres above the open sea surface, crossing three small islands of the An Thoi Strait over approximately 15 minutes. The view from the gondola is across the southern Phu Quoc coast, the inner archipelago, and — on clear days — the Cambodian coast to the northwest. The cable car return from the island is included.
items.imageAlt: Phu Quoc world's longest sea cable car 7.9km Vietnam

items.title: Four-Island Hopping Circuit + Aquatopia
items.body: The 4-island speedboat circuit departs An Thoi Harbour on day three: Coral Park snorkelling (1–5m coral reef, sea walking optional), May Rut Island beach (white sand, 45 minutes), Gam Ghi Island shallow coral (1–3m, visible from the surface), and Pineapple Island (Hon Thom) for the full day at Aquatopia Water Park with buffet lunch at Mango Restaurant (100+ dishes) and the Eagle Eye Observatory (360-degree view of the An Thoi Strait at 100m elevation). Return via the cable car.
items.imageAlt: Phu Quoc 4 island hopping snorkelling Aquatopia Vietnam

itinerary.heading: Your 5-Day Best of Phu Quoc Itinerary

days.title: Arrive Phu Quoc — Ho Quoc Pagoda & Night Market
days.description: Arrive at Phu Quoc International Airport. Transfer to hotel (20–30 minutes). Afternoon: Ho Quoc Pagoda (Truc Lam Ho Quoc) on the east coast — Buddhist complex built into a granite cliff above the sea, with staircase viewpoints at each level. Sanato Beach (Sunset Town) for afternoon swimming and the western sunset view. Duong Dong Night Market: fresh grilled seafood, Phu Quoc pepper crab, fish sauce tastings. Dinner at Night Market.
suggestedActivities: Ho Quoc Pagoda (cliffside temple, east coast)
Grilled seafood and pepper crab dinner

days.title: Starfish Island — Grand World Phu Quoc
days.description: Morning: Starfish Island (Bai Sao Bien, northern Phu Quoc — accessible by boat from the pier). Starfish visible in the shallows October–March — large coloured starfish in ankle-to-knee water depth. Transfer to Grand World Phu Quoc. Lunch. Grand World afternoon/evening: Venice Canal (gondola optional RM35), Bamboo Legend Vietnamese cultural zone (folk performances, lantern streets), Clock Tower, food street. Optional Teddy Bear Museum (RM35) and cultural show (RM48). Optional evening: Sunset Town pier (Kiss Bridge, ferris wheel). Dinner at Grand World food street or own arrangement.

days.title: 4-Island Hopping + Aquatopia + Cable Car
days.description: 07:00–08:30: Shuttle to An Thoi Harbour for 4-island speedboat circuit. Coral Park: snorkelling at 1–5m coral reef. Optional sea walking (own expense, approximately RM80). May Rut Island: white sand beach swimming (45 minutes). Gam Ghi Island: shallow 1–3m coral reef (good for non-snorkellers). Hon Thom (Pineapple Island): Aquatopia Water Park full day (all rides included), Eagle Eye Observatory, buffet lunch at Mango Restaurant (100+ dishes, included). 16:00: Return via world's longest sea cable car (7.9 km). Transfer back to hotel.
suggestedActivities: Aquatopia Water Park (all rides, full day)
Buffet lunch at Mango Restaurant (100+ dishes)
16:00 world's longest sea cable car return

days.title: Free Day — Optional VinWonders or Vinpearl Safari
days.description: Full free day at leisure. Optional VinWonders Phu Quoc (RM245 per person, own expense) — rides, 5D cinema, swimming lagoon, themed entertainment zones. Optional Vinpearl Safari (RM245 per person, own expense) — open wildlife park with free-roaming African and Asian animals. VinWonders + Safari Combo (RM405). Alternatively: Sao Beach (Bai Sao, rated one of the most beautiful beaches in Southeast Asia — white powder sand, turquoise water, 30 minutes from Duong Dong). Dinner at own arrangement.

days.title: Phu Quoc Free Morning — Depart
days.description: Free final morning on Phu Quoc. Options: Sao Beach for a last swim (15-minute taxi), Long Beach (Bai Truong) walk along the west coast resort strip, Phu Quoc fish sauce factory (traditional black-barrel fermented anchovy sauce — the best in Vietnam), or morning coffee at Duong Dong riverside. Transfer to Phu Quoc International Airport per agreed schedule.
suggestedActivities: Optional: Phu Quoc fish sauce factory

whyBook.heading: The Complete Phu Quoc Experience — By Malaysia's Trusted Agency Since 1988
whyBook.body: This five-day package covers every important Phu Quoc experience in the right sequence. The Starfish Island timing is seasonal (October to March) — Dhesu will advise whether starfish are visible based on your travel dates. Grand World is best in the evening — we schedule the visit to arrive as the lights and gondola begin. The island hopping is pre-booked so the speedboat departure is guaranteed. Dhesu has been arranging Vietnam island packages for Malaysian travellers since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. Phu Quoc packages are among our most popular Vietnam island itineraries. All speedboat and cable car bookings are pre-confirmed.

points.title: 35+ Years of Vietnam Experience
points.body: Since 1988, we have been sending Malaysian travellers to Vietnam. We know exactly when Phu Quoc's starfish are visible, when the cable car queue is shortest, and where to eat the best pepper crab.

points.title: Grand World + 4-Island + Cable Car All Included
points.body: Grand World Phu Quoc, 4-island snorkelling circuit, Aquatopia, Eagle Eye Observatory, buffet lunch, and the 7.9km cable car are all included. No additional ticketing on the island.

points.title: Pay for 3, 4th Goes Free
points.body: Groups of 4 travelling together pay for only 3 — the 4th person travels free. Note: starfish are only visible October–March. Ask about best season timing when booking.

sections.heading: 5 Day Best of Phu Quoc Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
The 5-day Best of Phu Quoc package covers every essential experience on Vietnam's most developed resort island. Phu Quoc is accessible from Kuala Lumpur via direct AirAsia flights (approximately 1.5 hours), making it one of the closest international beach destinations for Malaysian travellers.

Five days allows Phu Quoc to be experienced without rushing: the Ho Quoc cliff temple and Night Market on arrival, Starfish Beach and Grand World Venice canal on day two, the 4-island snorkelling circuit and Aquatopia Water Park with the world-record cable car return on day three, VinWonders or Vinpearl Safari on day four, and a final beach morning on day five. The 'Pay for 3, 4th free' promotion makes this the best-value Phu Quoc package for groups.

faq.heading: Questions About the 5-Day Best of Phu Quoc Package

items.question: When can I see the starfish at Starfish Beach?
items.answer: Starfish are visible at Starfish Beach (Bai Sao Bien, northern Phu Quoc) from October to March only. Outside these months, the warm current that draws the starfish to the shallow sandflat is absent. Dhesu will confirm whether starfish are expected based on your travel dates. If your visit falls outside October–March, the morning can be replaced with an alternative activity.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Vietnam?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders enter Vietnam visa-free for up to 30 days. Phu Quoc International Airport receives direct flights from Kuala Lumpur (AirAsia). No advance visa required.

items.question: What is Grand World Phu Quoc?
items.answer: Grand World is a large riverside entertainment complex on Phu Quoc's northern coast, opened in 2021. The main attraction is a recreated Venice canal with gondola rides (optional, RM35 per boat). Adjacent zones include Bamboo Legend (Vietnamese cultural performances and lantern alleys), a food street, Clock Tower, Teddy Bear Museum (optional, RM35), and a cultural performance venue (optional, RM48). Grand World is busiest and most atmospheric after sunset.

items.question: How does the 5-day package compare to the 4-day package?
items.answer: The 5-day package adds Grand World Phu Quoc and Starfish Island on day two, plus an additional night on the island. The 4-day package (pkgid 2411) covers Ho Quoc Pagoda, 4-island hopping with cable car, and the VinWonders optional day — without Grand World or Starfish Beach. The 5-day is the better choice if you want to see everything Phu Quoc has, especially if travelling October–March when starfish are visible.
